etymology.description: 口(口) (kǒu, 'mouth') + 八(八) (bā, 'divide') -> dispersal of breath -> borrowed for 'four'
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted by 亖, representing four lines, the character later evolved to include 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') enclosing 八 (bā, 'divide') to symbolize a dispersal of breath. This representation was adapted over time for the numerical value 'four', borrowing phonetically from the original pronunciation linked to breath dispersion. The character was standardized during the Qin dynasty to its current form to avoid confusion with 'two' and 'three' in vertical script.

examples.chinese: 我有四本书(我有四本書)。
四个人在公园里散步(四個人在公園裡散步)。
examples.pinyin: Sì gè rén zài gōngyuán lǐ sànbù.
examples.english: Four people are walking in the park.

examples.chinese: 比赛将在四点开始(比賽將在四點開始)。
examples.pinyin: Bǐsài jiāng zài sì diǎn kāishǐ.
examples.english: The match will start at four o'clock.
